front: What was the amyloid hypothesis and how has it evolved?
back: 1984: Aβ discovered in Alzheimer's plaques. Hypothesis: Aβ accumulation causes AD. Evolution: 1) Oligomers (not plaques) are neurotoxic, 2) Tau pathology is downstream, 3) Inflammation plays key role, 4) Anti-Aβ drugs (aducanumab, lecanemab) show modest effects, 5) Multi-target approaches emerging.
